summary: Audience‑sourced tape captures a solid 1995 Vegas show with a thunderous Truckin → Drums burst and an epic Morning Dew, though it has occasional lulls.
review: The performance is generally praised for its high energy—highlighting the Race Is On, a monster Truckin into Drums sequence, and one of the best late‑show Morning Dews of the era. The recording is an audience capture (no soundboard) that sounds decent enough to enjoy, but lacks the crispness of a board mix. A few reviewers note minor dips (the Samba section) and occasional monitor‑feed quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show highly (average ~4.3) and describe the tape as decent, yielding a weighted overall rating around 4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No soundboard available; audience tape is listenable with reasonable clarity but occasional monitor‑feed artifacts and no pristine mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, upbeat opening (Half Step, The Race Is On) followed by a high‑energy Truckin → Drums burst; the second set meanders a bit before delivering an outstanding Morning Dew that lifts the show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Had some monitor/sound‑check issues and was ‘off night’ according to one reviewer, but his presence still energized the crowd.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (and Mickey) are praised for a thunderous, heavy pounding during the Truckin → Drums segment.
